MessageBubble.expertsSectionDesc: These parliamentarians were identified as the most competent voices on the topic and used as priority sources to build the response.
MessageBubble.expertsTooltip: Before retrieving citations, the system identifies the most authoritative parliamentarians on the topic. Authority is calculated on 6 factors: floor speeches, legislative acts, committee membership, profession, education and institutional role.
MessageBubble.citationsTooltip: Parliamentary speeches retrieved from the database via hybrid semantic search (vector + full-text), from parliamentarians selected for authority on the topic.
MessageBubble.balanceTooltip: Percentage representation of citations used in the response between majority and opposition. A high score indicates a balanced response.
MessageBubble.hqTooltip: 3 response variants are generated at different temperatures, then an LLM judge selects the best for quality and balance.
MessageBubble.compassTooltip: 2D map of the ideological positioning of parliamentary groups on the topic, calculated by analyzing speech content along thematic axes extracted automatically.

ProgressIndicator.objectiveText: Generate a balanced analysis of the positions of all parliamentary groups, with verified citations from floor speeches.

ProgressIndicator.tourSemanticSearchDesc: Find speeches by concept, not just exact keywords.
ProgressIndicator.tourExpertsPerTopicDesc: Identifies the parliamentarians with the most authority on each subject.
ProgressIndicator.tourIdeologicalCompassDesc: Visualize the positioning of parliamentary groups on any topic.

ProgressIndicator.tourCommitteesDesc: Discover which committee is responsible for each topic addressed.

step1.label: Query analysis
step1.whyDescription: We analyze your question to identify the main topic and classify it correctly. This allows directing the search toward the most relevant committees and parliamentarians.

step2.label: Committees
step2.whyDescription: We identify the most relevant parliamentary committee for the requested topic. Committees are the heart of legislative work: this is where topics are discussed in detail.

step3.label: Authoritative sources
step3.whyDescription: Before retrieving any citation, the system identifies the most authoritative parliamentarians on the topic. Authority is calculated on 6 criteria: floor speeches, legislative acts, committee membership, profession, education and institutional role. Only the most competent voices become priority sources.

step4.label: Speeches
step4.whyDescription: We retrieve speeches from parliamentarians identified as authoritative. This ensures that citations are not random, but come from those who have demonstrated real expertise on the topic.

step5.label: Statistics
step5.whyDescription: We calculate the representation percentages of majority and opposition in the speeches found. This allows us to measure and guarantee the balance of the response.

step6.label: Ideological Compass
step6.whyDescription: We analyze the ideological positioning of parliamentary groups on the specific topic, mapping positions on axes of an ideological compass.

step7.label: Generation
step7.whyDescription: We generate the final balanced synthesis integrating all collected data: speeches, experts, statistics and ideological positioning, with citations verified from parliamentary speeches.

step8.label: Saving
step8.whyDescription: We complete the final verification of the response, validating citations and saving the result to history for future consultation.

ExpertCard.whyAuthoritative: Why is this an authoritative source?
ExpertCard.authorityCriteria: Authority on the topic is calculated on 6 institutional and parliamentary criteria.

WelcomeScreen.title: What does Parliament think about every topic?
WelcomeScreen.subtitle: Statements from the <bold>most authoritative</bold> parliamentarians on each topic, balanced between majority and opposition with verifiable citations.
WelcomeScreen.topicQuery: What is the position of parliamentary groups on the topic: {topic}?

Settings.title: System Configuration
Settings.description: Changes take effect immediately but are temporary — restart the server or Reload to restore YAML file values.
Settings.invalidJson: Invalid JSON format. Fix the syntax errors.

profession.label: Profession
profession.description: Contribution of pre-parliamentary profession to the authority score. A parliamentarian with a medical or legal background has specific expertise for sector queries.

education.label: Education
education.description: Contribution of academic qualifications to the authority score. Specialized degrees and PhDs in relevant fields increase the weight.

committee.label: Committees
committee.description: Contribution of membership in thematically relevant parliamentary committees relative to the query. The higher the semantic relevance, the greater the weight.

acts.label: Acts
acts.description: Contribution of the number of parliamentary acts submitted (laws, motions, questions), weighted by recency via exponential time decay.

interventions.label: Speeches
interventions.description: Contribution of the number of floor speeches weighted by recency. Active and recent parliamentarians get higher scores than inactive ones.

role.label: Role
role.description: Contribution of institutional role (President, Minister, Group Leader, Undersecretary, etc.). Top roles receive an authority boost.

relevance.label: Relevance
relevance.description: Weight of the cosine similarity between query embedding and chunk embedding. This is the primary signal of thematic relevance.

diversity.label: Diversity
diversity.description: Penalizes chunks from the same speaker to prevent a single parliamentarian from dominating results, ensuring variety of voices.

coverage.label: Coverage
coverage.description: Rewards representation of multiple parliamentary groups in the final ranking, favoring multi-party perspectives.

authority.label: Authority
authority.description: Re-weights chunks based on the parliamentarian's authority score (expertise, role, acts). Note: the actual authority ranking happens post-merge.

salience.label: Salience
salience.description: Prefers politically substantive texts over procedural ones (e.g., 'Thank you, Mr. President, you may speak').

modelInfo.analyst: Stage 1: decomposes the query into thematic claims per party. Structured and repetitive task — gpt-4o-mini is sufficient and cost-effective.
modelInfo.writer: Stage 2: writes sections for each parliamentary group from retrieved chunks. Requires high narrative quality and ideological coherence.
modelInfo.integrator: Stage 3: integrates sections into a coherent and balanced text. Stage 4 (Citation Surgeon) is deterministic and does not use LLM.

retrieval.title: Information Retrieval
retrieval.description: Parameters for the two search channels and the result merger
retrieval.topKResultsInfo: Maximum number of chunks retrieved by semantic similarity (embedding). Higher values increase recall but also increase latency and computational cost.
retrieval.similarityThresholdInfo: Minimum cosine similarity threshold for the dense channel. Chunks with lower similarity are discarded before ranking. Typical range: 0.20–0.45.
retrieval.minLexicalMatchInfo: Minimum number of EuroVoc keywords that must match lexically to include a parliamentary act in the graph search. Value 1 = one keyword is enough.
retrieval.semanticThresholdInfo: Semantic similarity threshold for EuroVoc matching. Controls how closely the EuroVoc concept must semantically match the query. Typical range: 0.35–0.55.
retrieval.graphChunkThresholdInfo: Minimum similarity threshold for chunks retrieved via graph traversal. Prevents inclusion of off-topic chunks retrieved only through co-signature of relevant acts.
retrieval.maxActsPerQueryInfo: Maximum number of parliamentary acts to retrieve per query via the graph channel. Limits the computational load of Neo4j graph traversal.

authority.title: Authority Score
authority.description: Weights and thresholds for calculating parliamentarian authority scores
authority.halfLifeActsInfo: Half-life in days for temporal decay of parliamentary acts. An act submitted 'half_life' days ago is worth half of a current one. Low values favor recent activity.
authority.halfLifeInterventionsInfo: Half-life in days for temporal decay of floor speeches. Recent speeches weigh more than older ones.
authority.relevanceThresholdsDescription: Minimum cosine similarity between the act/speech embedding and the query. Items below the threshold are excluded from the count, regardless of quantity.
authority.actsRelevanceThresholdInfo: Minimum cosine similarity between the act description and the query to count it toward authority. Irrelevant acts do not contribute to the score. Typical range: 0.20–0.35.
authority.interventionsRelevanceThresholdInfo: Minimum cosine similarity between the speech embedding and the query to count it. Off-topic speeches do not increase the parliamentarian's score. Typical range: 0.20–0.35.
authority.maxComponentContributionInfo: Maximum contribution (cap) that a single component can make to the final authority score. Prevents a single factor (e.g., number of speeches) from completely dominating the score.

generation.title: Generation
generation.description: LLM models and parameters for the 4-stage generation pipeline
generation.maxTokenOutputInfo: Maximum number of tokens generated per response. Increase for longer responses, decrease to contain costs.
generation.temperatureInfo: Model creativity/randomness. 0 = deterministic and precise, 1 = creative and variable. For analytical texts, 0.2–0.4 is optimal.
generation.topPInfo: Nucleus sampling: considers only tokens with cumulative probability up to Top-P. 1.0 = no filtering. Lower to 0.9–0.95 for more focused output.
generation.enabledInfo: Provides the writer with a summary of the parliamentary group's overall position (top N chunks) before writing the section. Improves ideological coherence of selected citations.
generation.maxChunksBriefInfo: Number of chunks included in the position brief. More chunks provide more context but increase input tokens and latency.
generation.charsPerChunkInfo: Maximum characters per chunk in the brief. Truncates long chunks to keep the brief compact.
generation.contextCharsInfo: Context characters shown for each evidence in the brief (text surrounding the citation). Helps the writer understand the tone of the speech.
generation.crossSynthesisInfo: Enables the 'Cross-Party Analysis' section (Stage 3.5): identifies cross-party patterns, convergences and divergences between parliamentary groups. Increases response length and cost.
generation.noEvidenceMessageInfo: Text shown in the parliamentary group section when no relevant speeches are present in the corpus for that query.

queryRewriting.title: Query Rewriting
queryRewriting.description: Automatic expansion of short queries before retrieval
queryRewriting.enabledInfo: When enabled, short queries are reformulated and enriched with related terms before being sent to retrieval. Improves recall for ambiguous or overly concise queries.
queryRewriting.modelInfo: LLM model used to rewrite queries. gpt-4o-mini is recommended: fast, cost-effective and sufficient for this structured task.
queryRewriting.maxWordsInfo: Maximum word threshold: queries with a word count equal to or less than this are rewritten. Already descriptive (longer) queries are passed directly to retrieval.

Timeline.browseDescription: Browse parliamentary sessions, debates, and speaker positions
Timeline.shortDebateNoSummary: Not enough speeches to generate a summary.
Timeline.askQuestionTemplate: What positions were expressed in the debate on {debateTitle} on {date}?
